 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.3","title":"[Metrics] Unit tests for rule metrics queries","description":"# Task: Unit tests for rule metrics queries

## Purpose
Validate correctness of rule-level analytics (counts, allowlist rate, severity
aggregation) with deterministic fixtures.

## Requirements
- Fixture history DB or in-memory data
- Tests for query correctness
- Ensure stats match expected totals

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover multiple rule IDs and packs
- Clear failure diagnostics

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.4","title":"[Metrics] E2E tests for stats --rules (pretty + JSON)","description":"# Task: E2E tests for stats --rules (pretty + JSON)

## Purpose
Ensure the CLI output for rule metrics is stable and correct in both formats.

## Requirements
- Fixture data with known rule counts
- Validate pretty output includes totals and top N
- Validate JSON output schema and values
- Verbose logging on failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests pass consistently
- Output formats are deterministic
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:28:54.676051814-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:30:37.626654988-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T02:30:37.626654988-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 15 comprehensive E2E tests for dcg stats --rules:
- Pretty output: header, all rules, totals, rule count, limit, trend indicators
- JSON output: valid structure, required fields, totals, rule values, limit, days filter, trend validation
- Empty database: friendly message
- All tests pass with isolated temp databases and known fixture data (12 hits, 3 rules, 3 overrides)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:28:54.677184958-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7ug8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:29:05.791754982-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:29:08.510976875-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.5","title":"[Metrics] Unit tests for rule_id capture in history","description":"# Task: Unit tests for rule_id capture in history

## Purpose
Guarantee that rule_id is reliably recorded for all deny/warn outcomes in history.

## Requirements
- Unit tests that exercise at least one core rule and verify stored rule_id
- Tests for allowlist override cases (ensure original rule_id recorded)

## Acceptance Criteria
- History entries include correct rule_id
- Tests fail with clear diagnostics when missing

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.6","title":"[Metrics] Time-series trending and historical analysis","description":"# Task: Time-Series Trending

## Background
Track how rule hit patterns change over time to identify trends.

## Implementation
- Store timestamped hit counts in history DB
- Calculate week-over-week changes
- Identify increasing/decreasing trends
- Flag anomalies (sudden spikes)

## CLI Output
```
dcg stats --rules --trend

Rule                  Hits (7d)  Trend    Change
git.force-push           12      ↑ +50%   (8 last week)
filesystem.rm-rf          5      ↓ -40%   (8 last week)
docker.prune              3      → stable
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Trending data calculated correctly
- Anomaly detection for spikes > 200%
- JSON output includes trend data","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:38:03.273895806-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T15:38:03.273895806-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:38:03.27533686-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1g6","title":"Replace evaluator mock tests with real pack fixtures","description":"## Objective

Eliminate mock pattern structs in `src/evaluator.rs` tests by exercising the real pack registry + pattern types.

## Scope

- Identify mock types (`MockSafePattern`, `MockDestructivePattern`) and replace with real `packs::SafePattern`/`packs::DestructivePattern`.
- Use minimal real packs or a small test-only pack registry with real regexes.
- Ensure tests verify decision outputs and match metadata (pack_id/pattern_name/reason).

## Notes

- Avoid fakes: use actual `REGISTRY` where practical, or create a real `Pack` instance (not a mock) with real regexes.
- Keep performance-sensitive behavior (quick reject, normalization) covered via real inputs.

## Acceptance criteria

- No `Mock*Pattern` types remain in evaluator tests.
- Coverage of evaluator decisions stays or improves.
- Tests remain deterministic and do not depend on external state.
","notes":"Attempted to replace mock patterns but encountering file modification conflicts - appears another agent may be actively editing evaluator.rs. Will defer and work on another task.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:29:29.36612941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T02:49:18.470718057-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T02:49:18.470718057-05:00","close_reason":"Mock pattern types removed from evaluator tests. Also fixed heredoc allowlist language aliases and project path matching bugs.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1g6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-xqv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:31:46.005173956-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","title":"Epic: Explainability, UX, and Operations","description":"# Epic: Explainability, UX, and Operations

## Why this epic exists

Even a correct guard will occasionally block or warn. When that happens, the tool must:

- Explain *what* matched (pack + pattern), *where* it matched (execution context), and *why* it’s considered dangerous.
- Provide clear, low-friction escape hatches (manual run guidance, allowlisting, warn/log-only modes).
- Produce logs that allow teams to diagnose false positives and tune configuration.

Without this, users experience “annoying and disruptive” interruptions and will disable the guard.

## Goals

1. **Explainability:** a human can quickly understand the decision without reading source.
2. **Ergonomics for coding agents:** easy to test, easy to allowlist, easy to see what would be blocked.
3. **Operational knobs:** block vs warn vs log-only; per-pack enable/disable; per-pattern allowlist.
4. **High-quality logging:** deterministic, machine-parseable (optional), and privacy-conscious.

## Non-goals

- Building a full policy language; keep configuration understandable.

## Success Criteria

- A user can run something like `dcg test --explain "..."` and see:
  - normalized command
  - execution contexts detected
  - packs considered/skipped (keyword gating)
  - the first blocking match with `pack_id` + `pattern_name`
- The deny/warn message includes a stable identifier that can be allowlisted without copying regexes.
- Logging can be enabled to collect blocked events with timestamps, pack/pattern IDs, and command (with optional redaction).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:57:39.637876513-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:13:42.949518995-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T14:13:42.949518995-05:00","close_reason":"All subtasks complete: 1gt.1-7 (explain, allowlist, decision modes, logging, suggestions, doctor), 1gt.8 (simulate). Explainability, UX, and Operations epic fully delivered."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1","title":"Add dcg explain (full decision trace; pretty/json/compact)","description":"## Objective

Ship an explainability mode that makes `dcg` trustworthy and debuggable:

- `dcg explain "<command>"` (primary UX)
- `dcg test --explain "<command>"` (alias for existing CLI habits)

Explain mode must show exactly why a command was allowed/blocked, with enough detail that users can resolve false positives quickly (goal: under 2 minutes).

## Why this matters

When a hook blocks a command, users immediately ask:

- What matched?
- Where did it match (executed vs data span)?
- Which packs were considered vs skipped (keyword gating)?
- What is the stable rule ID I should allowlist (without copying regex)?
- What safe alternative should I use instead?

If we can’t answer these questions quickly, users disable the guard.

## Hard constraints

- Hook path must never hang: no interactive prompts, no waiting for stdin.
- Fast path must stay fast: explain tracing is opt-in and must not add overhead when disabled.
- Deterministic: the same input yields the same trace and rule ID.

## Proposed UX

### Pretty (default)

Box/sectioned output designed for human scanning. Includes:

- raw input command
- normalized command
- decision (ALLOW/WARN/DENY)
- latency breakdown (per stage)
- execution-context spans (executed vs data)
- allowlist evaluation (entries checked + match, if any)
- pack evaluation (packs checked vs skipped; first match)
- stable rule id (`pack_id:pattern_name`)
- matched span (byte offsets) + matched text preview
- suggestions (safe alternatives + "how to allow safely" command examples)

### JSON

Machine-readable output for CI tooling, bug reports, and snapshot testing.

Minimum fields:

- `command`, `normalized_command`
- `decision`
- `total_duration_us`
- `steps[]`: `{ name, duration_us, details }`
- `context.spans[]`: `{ start, end, kind, text_preview? }`
- `pack_summary`: enabled packs, skipped packs (keyword gating)
- `match`: `{ pack_id, pattern_name, rule_id, reason, matched_span }`
- `allowlist`: `{ layers_checked, matched_entry? }`
- `suggestions[]`: `{ kind, text, command? }`

### Compact

Single-line output suitable for logs:

- `DENY core.git:reset-hard "git reset --hard" — <reason> (847us)`

## Technical design (trace model)

Add a trace data model that can be enabled in CLI-only modes:

- `ExplainTrace { decision, steps[], match_identity?, suggestions[] }`
- Each `TraceStep` records:
  - stage name
  - duration
  - stage-specific details

Trace steps should cover (at minimum):

1. Input parsing (hook JSON vs CLI input)
2. Pack-aware keyword gating (global quick reject)
3. Normalization (wrappers, path stripping)
4. Execution-context classification (spans)
5. Allowlist checks (project/user/system)
6. Pack evaluation (safe then destructive; which packs were skipped)
7. Policy decision layer (deny vs warn vs log-only)
8. Output formatting summary

Key implementation constraint:

- When tracing is disabled, the evaluator should not allocate or capture extra data.

## Dependencies

Explain mode becomes valuable only when these are in place:

- deterministic pack evaluation + stable match identity (`pack_id` + `pattern_name`)
- shared evaluator used by both CLI and hook
- execution-context classification (or at least the safe string-arg registry)
- allowlisting by rule ID (so explain can recommend an allowlist command)

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg explain` works for both blocked and allowed commands.
- For a blocked command, explain shows the exact `pack_id:pattern_name` and matched span.
- For a false-positive-like command (e.g., `bd create --description="rm -rf"`), explain explicitly shows the string span as data, and that matching was skipped for it.
- Output is deterministic (golden tests stable across runs).
- Hook path remains unchanged (no interactive prompt; no output for allow).

## Test plan

- Golden/snapshot tests for:
  - pretty output (stable sections + key fields)
  - JSON output schema
  - compact single-line output
- Determinism tests: same input run 100 times yields the same match identity.
- Latency sanity: explain mode can be slower, but must remain bounded and must not affect hook mode when disabled.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:04:20.427652456-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T13:29:23.672684619-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T13:29:23.672684619-05:00","close_reason":"All subtasks completed: trace model, pretty/compact/json formatters, and CLI wiring","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:04:20.428791912-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:37.096250877-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:42.129983029-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:47.162937778-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.6","type":"supersedes","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:55:15.614236806-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
